title: Loan requirements
description: Practice understanding the qualifications and information needed to secure a loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Getting a loan usually requires meeting requirements established by the lender.

**Which of these factors are essential for getting a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Getting a loan usually requires meeting requirements established by the lender.

**Which of these are determining factors for getting a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Select the appropriate options from each dropdown menu that best describe how lenders look at loan applications.**

To approve a loan, lenders prefer that a person have a [[☃ dropdown 1]] credit score and a [[☃ dropdown 2]] debt-to-income ratio.
hints: A credit score shows lenders how well you've managed your money and used credit in the past. The higher the credit score, the more likely you are to get a loan.
Lenders prefer that a person have a high credit score.
Debt-to-income (DTI) ratio is a number that compares how much money you owe to how much money you make.
Lenders prefer that a person have a low debt to income ratio.
To approve a loan, lenders prefer that a person have a **high** credit score and a **low** debt-to-income ratio.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are applying for a small loan to buy some new furniture. After filling out an application, the bank lets you know that your debt-to-income (DTI) ratio is $0.2$, which is considered low.

**What is most likely to happen as a result of this DTI ratio?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Debt-to-income (DTI) ratio** is a number that compares how much money you owe to how much money you make.
If your DTI is $0.2$ that means that $20\%$ of your income goes to paying off your debts.

Lenders prefer that DTI be $0.3$ or *lower*, so a DTI of $0.2$ is good.

The *lower* the DTI, the *higher* the chance of approval.

choices.content: The bank will approve the loan.
The bank will deny the loan.
Nothing. Banks do not consider DTI ratio when giving loans.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are checking your account portal after applying for a loan.  The lender indicates you have a debt-to-income (DTI) ratio of $0.1$, which is considered low.

**What is most likely to happen as a result of this DTI ratio?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Debt-to-income (DTI) ratio** is a number that compares how much money you owe to how much money you make.
If your DTI is $0.2$ that means that $20\%$ of your income goes to paying off your debts.

Lenders prefer that DTI be $0.3$ or *lower*, so a DTI of $0.2$ is good.

The *lower* the DTI, the *higher* the chance of approval.

choices.content: You will have a high chance of being approved.
You will have a low chance of being approved.
The DTI is irrelevant to the loan decision process.

assessment_items.question_content:
A person applied for a loan and got denied. The lender quoted that they did not meet the DTI (debt-to-income) requirement.

**What does this mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Debt-to-income (DTI) ratio** is a number that compares how much money you owe to how much money you make.
If your DTI is $0.2$ that means that $20\%$ of your income goes to paying off your debts.

Lenders prefer that DTI be $0.3$ or *lower*, so a DTI of $0.2$ is good.

The *lower* the DTI, the *higher* the chance of approval.

choices.content: The DTI was below the national average.

assessment_items.question_content:
During a loan application, lenders will ask you to provide some personal information.

**Which of these are needed when applying for a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

During a loan application, lenders will ask you to provide some personal information.

**Which of these are needed when applying for a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

During a loan application, lenders will ask you to provide some personal information.

**Which of these are needed when applying for a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Certain lenders require collateral before approving a loan.

**What does having *collateral* mean when applying for a loan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It refers to another person who is applying for the loan with you.
It means having a high credit score.
It indicates a good debt-to-income ratio.
It's a valuable asset that the lender can take if you can't repay the loan.

assessment_items.question_content:
Certain lenders require collateral before approving a loan.

**What type of loan requires collateral?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Certain lenders require collateral before approving a loan.

**What type of loans do not require collateral?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
